problem_statement: LLMs frequently misuse library APIs in generated/completed code (wrong method selection, invalid parameters, hallucinated or intent-mismatched calls), and existing detection and program-repair techniques fail to reliably catch or fix these misuses at the scale of millions of APIs.
magnitude_evidence: GPT-4 generated code with API misuses in 62% of cases when tested on 18 widely used Java APIs [Zhong & Wang]; another study found LLMs misused APIs in approximately half of security-related Java tasks [Mousavi et al.]
insufficiency_evidence: "Even when misuse is detected correctly, rule- and program-analysis-based automatic program repair (APR) techniques often fail to fix misused APIs due to their limited semantic understanding"; hallucination accounts for up to 43.3% of parameter misuses (StarCoder, Java), and naive prompting baselines had a refusal rate of 0% (GPT-4o)
solution_mechanisms: API misuse taxonomy (intent, hallucination, missing, redundant)
static analysis to localize API positions
